content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
August 27, 1987 
Douglas Hickling, Assistant County Counsel 
Alameda County 
1221 Oak street, Fourth Floor 
Oakland, CA 94612 
Dear Mr. Hickling: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-214 
You have requested advice regarding the applicability of 
section 84308 to the Alameda County Transportation Authority. 
QUESTION 
Are members of the Alameda County Board of Supervisors 
subject to the provisions of section 84308 of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act) Y when sitting as ex-officio members of 
the Alameda County Transportation Authority? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes, the supervisors are subject to the provisions of 
section 84308 when serving on the Alameda County Transportation 
Authority. 
FACTS 
The Alameda County Transportation Authority is comprised of 
the five members of the Alameda County Board of Supervisors, 
and four elected city officials. 
One of the elected city 
officials is designated by the mayor of Oakland and the other 
three are named by the Alameda County Mayor's Conference. 
The 
members of the board of supervisors are required to serve on 
the authority as part of their official duties. 
They 
constitute a quorum of the transportation authority. 
ANALYSIS 
section 84308 deals specifically with campaign 
contributions and government decision-making by officials 
serving on a specific type of public agency. 
The law applies 
to board members, commissioners or other individuals who serve 
Y 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


Douglas Hickling 
August 27, 1987 
Page 2 
on state or local government agencies defined in section 
84308(a) (3), and who make decisions in proceedings which 
involve licenses, permits, or other entitlements for use. 
Section 84308(a) (3) exempts from the definition of 
"agency": 
" ... local governmental agencies whose members are-
directly elected by the voters .... " 
Section 84308(a) (3) goes 
on to more narrowly refine the exemption by adding: 
"However, 
this section applies to any person who is a member of an 
exempted agency but is acting as a voting member of another 
agency." 
Regulation 18438.I(b) further clarifies the local 
government bodies which are exempted from the definition of 
"agency" for purposes of Section 84308: 
(b) The exemption for the officers of local governmental 
agencies who are directly elected by the voters applies 
only to agencies whose entire membership consists of 
officers directly elected by the voters to serve on that 
agency. 
The members of the Alameda County Board of Supervisors are 
directly elected by the voters. 
Thus when the board of 
supervisors sits as a body, in its entirety, without additional 
members, the supervisors are exempt from section 84308. 
(See 
Hopkins Advice Letter, No. 83-009, copy enclosed.) 
This is the 
case when the board of supervisors operates as the Alameda 
County Flood Control and Water Conservation District. 
However, when the board of supervisors is required to serve 
in its entirety with other individuals, to constitute a public 
agency, the supervisors are subject to the requirements of 
Section 84308. 
(See Strauss Advice Letter, No. 83-008, copy 
enclosed). 
Thus, all members of the transportation authority 
are subject to section 84308. 
I trust this clarifies application of the law to the 
circumstances you outlined. 
If you have any further questions, 
please don't hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
LS: DGM: j aj 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Division 


COUNTY 
COUNrSE 
.-
FOURTH FLOOR, ADMINISTRATION BUILDING, 1221 OAK STREET 
OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94612 
State of California 
Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Mr. Robert Leidigh 
Sir or Madam: 
August 7, 1987 
ICHARD j, MOORE 
COUNTY 
Several months ago your office informally advised us by telephone that, 
when 
Alameda County Board 
Supervisors acts 
its ex-officio capacity 
as the Alameda County Flood Control and Water Conservation District, 
is not 
an agency subject to the provisions of section 84308 
the Government Code 
because all of its members are directly elected by the voters. 
would appreciate your response to 
somewhat similar question: 
members 
the Board of Supervisors subject to the 
provisions of section 84308 when sitting as ex-officio members 
of 
Alameda County Transportation Authority? 
Note: 
The Authority is comprised of all five members 
the 
Board of Supervisors and 
four elected city officials, one 
designated by the Mayor of Oakland and the other 
named by 
the Alameda County Mayor's Conference. 
The members of the Board 
of Supervisors have no choice but to serve on the Authority 
they constitute a quorum. 
Very truly yours, 
RICHARD J. :MOORE

subject to the provisions of section 84308 of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act) Y when sitting as ex-officio members of 
the Alameda County Transportation Authority?

CONCLUSION: Yes, the supervisors are subject to the provisions of 
section 84308 when serving on the Alameda County Transportation 
Authority.
